title: Prosthetics Artistry in Guillermo del Toro's Frankenstein
text: Makeup artist Mike Hill created intricate prosthetics for Jacob Elordi's role as the Creature in del Toro's adaptation now streaming on Netflix. Hill's approach prioritized humanity over monstrosity, wanting to stay away from classical zombies or anything like that. Rather than creating a grotesque monster, the prosthetics were designed to reflect a being assembled from battlefield corpses with visible stitching and varying skin tones. The full-face prosthetics covered nearly all of Elordi's features, leaving only the tip of the nose, the upper lip and his chin exposed. Hill subtly matured the then-26-year-old actor's appearance by adding a stronger nose and brow—key features associated with Frankenstein's creature but applied with restraint. One eye featured a brown contact lens to make it appear larger, while del Toro advised against zombie-like eye sockets to maintain focus on the character's emotional humanity. Hill noted that the team applied the full body probably about 20 times, but he never complained once, describing how Elordi used the extensive makeup process as an opportunity to embody the character's emotional depth.
